articles.summary: If you're building or scaling a business, achieving compliance with SOC 2, ISO 27001 or other in-demand frameworks can unlock major growth for your company and establish customer trust.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC HAS A FAST NEW AI MODEL — AND A CLEVER NEW WAY TO INTERACT WITH CHATBOTS
articles.summary: Anthropic's newest model, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, is reportedly equal to or better than OpenAI's GPT-4o and Google's Gemini across a wide variety of tasks. The model performs well in benchmarks, suggesting that Anthropic has built a legitimate competitor in the space. Claude 3.5 Sonnet is available now to web and iOS users and will be available to developers as well. Anthropic is introducing a new feature called Artifacts that will allow users to see, interact with, and edit the results of their Claude requests.

articles.title: DELL SAID RETURN TO THE OFFICE OR ELSE—NEARLY HALF OF WORKERS CHOSE “OR ELSE”
articles.summary: Dell announced a return-to-office initiative earlier this year that involved workers having to classify themselves as remote or hybrid. Those who chose to classify themselves as remote had to agree that they could no longer be promoted or hired into new roles within the company. Nearly 50% of Dell's workforce opted to stay remote. Many interviewed employees admitted that they were looking for work at other companies that weren't trying to force employees back to the office. Many other companies are struggling to restore in-person work.

articles.title: SPACEX UNVEILS BACKPACK-SIZED ‘STARLINK MINI' SATELLITE INTERNET ANTENNA FOR $599
articles.summary: SpaceX is rolling out a mobile option for its satellite internet customers. Starlink Mini is a backpack-sized kit designed to provide high-speed, low-latency internet on the go. SpaceX is offering a limited number of the antennas for $599 in an early access release. Service for the Starlink Mini will be effectively $150 a month and capped at 50 gigabytes of data per month. SpaceX has not announced when Starlink Mini deliveries will begin.

articles.title: GILEAD'S TWICE-YEARLY SHOT TO PREVENT HIV SUCCEEDS IN LATE-STAGE TRIAL
articles.summary: Gilead's lenacapavir was 100% effective in preventing HIV in roughly 2,000 women in a late-stage trial. The results will need to be replicated before Gilead can seek approval from the Food and Drug Administration. Lenacapavir could be on the market as soon as late 2025 if trial results continue to be positive.

articles.title: 🎫 GET TICKETS FOR DEVRELCON NEW YORK WITH A SPECIAL DISCOUNT OFFER FOR TLDR READERS
articles.summary: Looking to hone your craft, advance your DevRel career, or connect with the wider dev tool community? DevRelCon [10] is the place for you. Sessions will cover practical topics [10] like metrics and ROI, managing up and down, community management, content creation, career paths, open source, improving and scaling using AI tools, and much more.

articles.title: Grab your $100 OFF discounted tickets at hackp.ac/TLDR100OFF
NEW IOS 18 API BRINGS AIRPODS SETUP EXPERIENCE TO THIRD-PARTY ACCESSORIES
articles.summary: AccessorySetupKit is a new API in iOS 18 that gives third-party accessories the same setup experience as Apple accessories such as AirPods and AirTag. Devices running iOS 18 will show a pairing confirmation popup when the right app detects a compatible accessory. The system will automatically handle all the Bluetooth or Wi-Fi connectivity required by the accessory. A video showing what the new feature will look like is available.

articles.title: ES-TOOLKIT
articles.summary: es-toolkit is a JavaScript utility library with a small bundle size and strong type annotations. It offers a variety of everyday utility functions with modern implementations. es-toolkit is designed with performance in mind and reduces JavaScript code by up to 97% compared to other libraries. It has built-in TypeScript support with straightforward types and useful type guards.

articles.title: WHY AMERICANS AREN'T BUYING MORE EVS
articles.summary: Electric vehicles are still too expensive to be accessible to millions of Americans. High prices, high interest rates, and concerns over driving range and charging infrastructure have chilled buyers' enthusiasm, even among those who consider themselves green. The US government has set policies that hit Chinese manufacturers of cars, batteries, and other components with punitive taxes. It also restricts federal tax incentives for consumers buying Chinese products. While the laws aim to allow the US to develop its own supply chains, analysts say such protectionism will result in higher EV prices for US consumers in the meantime and slow down EV adoption in the US.

articles.title: ASK HN: COULD AI BE A DOT COM SIZED BUBBLE? (HACKER NEWS THREAD)
NEW YORK BANS ‘ADDICTIVE FEEDS' FOR TEENS
articles.summary: New York has introduced two new bills - one will require parental consent for social media companies to use addictive feeds powered by recommendation algorithms on people under 18 and the other would limit data collection on minors without consent and restrict the sale of such information.

articles.title: TIKTOK MAKES ITS FIRST AMENDMENT CASE
articles.summary: TikTok claims that the government didn't adequately consider viable alternatives before charging ahead with a law that could ban the platform in the US.

articles.title: HOW APPLE IS WINNING THE FINANCIAL SERVICES GAME
APPLE INTELLIGENCE AND AI MAXIMALISM
articles.summary: Apple's AI strategy points to large language models being commodity infrastructure, not platforms or products.

articles.title: GOOGLE'S AI OVERVIEWS RELYING MUCH LESS ON REDDIT
articles.summary: Google has apparently toned down Reddit's influence on search results that use AI Overviews and significantly dropped the number of delivered AI Overview results overall.

articles.title: HOW SMALL CLAIMS COURT BECAME META'S CUSTOMER SERVICE HOTLINE
articles.summary: Users of Meta's services who have been unable to get help from real humans through normal channels of recourse are using the court system instead.
